name: Bahia (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the blood of those who fought for independence

description: Four equal horizontal stripes of white and red alternating, with a blue canton in the upper hoist bearing a white equilateral triangle.
history: Designed by Deocleciano Ramos and adopted as the flag of the State of Bahia in 1889, shortly after the proclamation of the Brazilian Republic; the design draws on the symbolism of the Conjuracao Baiana (Revolt of the Tailors) of 1798.

features.symbolism: Four equal horizontal stripes alternating white and red, drawn from the 1798 Conjuracao Baiana movement
Blue canton charged with a white triangle, evoking the emblem of the Conjuracao Baiana
Recalls the masonic and republican triangle adopted by the Conjuracao Baiana of 1798, symbolising the Trinity, equality, liberty, and fraternity
